name: zero_layer_arch_satisfies_inv
code:
proof fn phi_1_zero_layer_arch_satisfies_inv(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: A zero-layer architecture is degenerate (no translation at all) but would satisfy the invariant since all forall-quantifiers are vacuously true

name: adjacent_entry_bases_collide
code:
proof fn phi_2_adjacent_entry_bases_collide(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i + 1 < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, (i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: Two adjacent entries at the same layer sharing the same base address would mean distinct page table slots map the same virtual address region

name: page_frame_size_zero_under_inv
code:
proof fn phi_3_page_frame_size_zero_under_inv(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size == 0,
{
}
reason: A page with a zero-size frame in a valid directory would represent a nonsensical mapping to no physical memory

name: update_invalid_keeps_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_4_update_invalid_keeps_mapping(d: Directory, j: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        j < d.num_entries(),
        d.entries[j as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.update(j, NodeEntry::Invalid).interp_of_entry(j) !== Map::<nat, PTE>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Replacing a Page entry with Invalid should erase the mapping at that slot; a non-empty interp would mean unmapping fails to remove the translation

name: nonzero_subdir_base_equals_parent
code:
proof fn phi_5_nonzero_subdir_base_equals_parent(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        i > 0,
        d.entries[i as int] is Directory,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr == d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: A non-first subdirectory sharing the parent's base_vaddr would mean distinct directory entries govern the same virtual address range, breaking isolation
